commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an official external PixVerse video-generation provider package with docs, catalog/install metadata, text-to-video and image-to-video implementation, PixVerse API region configuration, and 360P/540P video resolution support.

PR surface: Source +635, Tests +605, Docs +182, Config +64, Other +51. Total +1537 across 36 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a report of broken existing behavior. The changed behavior is covered by focused provider tests and live PixVerse proof in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review is required because the PR is draft, member-authored, protected by the `maintainer` label, and introduces official provider/config scope rather than a narrow repairable defect.

target.title: feat(pixverse): add external video provider
target.body:
## Summary
- add PixVerse as an official external provider package, `@openclaw/pixverse-provider`, with npm/ClawHub publish metadata, package-local shrinkwrap, plugin manifest/contract registration, generated plugin inventory/reference docs, and official external provider catalog metadata
- add PixVerse text-to-video and image-to-video inference, including local/remote image upload, async status polling, hosted video URL return, model mapping for `v6` and `c1`, and explicit capability metadata
- add `models.providers.pixverse.region` so operators can choose the International API or CN API, while keeping `models.providers.pixverse.baseUrl` and `models.providers.pixverse.request` as the explicit proxy/transport override path
- add shared 360P/540P video-resolution normalization so PixVerse resolutions are first-class in CLI/tool/SDK surfaces
- keep PixVerse out of the bundled core npm artifact by excluding `dist/extensions/pixverse/**` from the root package files list

## Historical context
- no dedicated PixVerse issue or PR turned up in Gitcrawl/live GitHub search
- related prior work: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/73038 added DeepInfra video fallback coverage using a PixVerse-hosted DeepInfra model
- related open work: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84549 is about DeepInfra dynamic catalog loading, not the official PixVerse API

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/pixverse/video-generation-provider.test.ts src/media-generation/runtime-shared.test.ts src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts` passed: 3 files, 67 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/plugin-npm-package-manifest.test.ts test/package-manager-config.test.ts src/plugins/provider-install-catalog.test.ts src/media-generation/provider-capabilities.contract.test.ts extensions/pixverse/plugin-registration.contract.test.ts extensions/pixverse/video-generation-provider.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts` passed: 9 files, 188 tests
- `node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check` passed
- `node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --check --package-dir extensions/pixverse` passed
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check` passed
- `git diff --check` passed
- direct live CN PixVerse smoke passed with a transient redacted API key and `models.providers.pixverse.region = "cn"`: text-to-video completed in 16.8s and image-to-video completed in 17.6s, each returning one hosted video URL
- live International PixVerse harness passed after billing was unblocked: text-to-video completed in 16.1s and image-to-video completed in 21.6s, each returning one video
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` clean on the pushed branch: no accepted/actionable findings
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "(git fetch --unshallow origin main 2>/dev/null || git fetch --depth=2000 origin main) && git fetch origin main:refs/remotes/origin/main && pnpm check:changed --base origin/main --head HEAD"` passed on AWS Crabbox: provider=aws, run=run_42f034979f2b, lease=cbx_827694fbb810, exit=0

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: PixVerse text-to-video and image-to-video can be installed as the official external PixVerse provider package; image references are uploaded to PixVerse before image-to-video creation, async status is polled with fresh PixVerse trace IDs and guarded OpenClaw provider transport, the video URL is returned, and operators can choose International or CN PixVerse API endpoints through provider config.
Real environment tested: mocked provider API locally; live International PixVerse API text-to-video and image-to-video; live CN PixVerse API text-to-video and image-to-video with `models.providers.pixverse.region = "cn"`; AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate for typecheck/lint/import-cycle/shrinkwrap guards.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest commands above, generated docs check above, package-local shrinkwrap check above, config schema check above, direct CN PixVerse smoke with a transient redacted `PIXVERSE_API_KEY`, previously completed International PixVerse live harness, autoreview command above, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` command above.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed on the pushed branch; generated plugin inventory/reference docs, config schema baseline, and PixVerse shrinkwrap checks passed; live International and CN PixVerse requests completed authenticated text-to-video and image-to-video generation with one generated video returned for each mode; autoreview returned clean; AWS Crabbox changed gate passed with run=run_42f034979f2b.
Observed result after fix: PixVerse request bodies, upload flow, polling flow, provider metadata, external package metadata, official external provider catalog entry, generated docs, resolution normalization, API region selection, provider request overrides, and guarded polling headers are covered by targeted tests; live PixVerse auth, generation submission, status polling, and video URL return are confirmed for both text-to-video and image-to-video on International and CN API surfaces.
What was not tested: PixVerse video-to-video, because this provider does not advertise video-to-video support; generated video downloads were not persisted or attached.
target.author: vincentkoc
